title: Judge Orders Federal Government to Submit ICE Agent Ross's Training and Personnel Files for Private Review
excerpt: A U.S. District Court judge ordered the federal government to turn over ICE agent Jonathan Ross's complete training and personnel files, along with all use-of-force policies, for in camera review — a rare judicial step underscoring the centrality of training records to the Minneapolis shooting investigation.
content:
On April 10, 2026, a U.S. District Court judge issued an order requiring the federal government to submit unredacted evidence from the Renee Good shooting — including ICE agent Jonathan Ross's complete training and personnel files — for private judicial review (in camera).

The order specifically enumerated Ross's 'complete training and personnel files' and 'ICE and Department of Homeland Security policies regarding use of force' as required submissions, signaling judicial concern that training records were central to evaluating whether Ross's actions were lawful.

  • The state of Minnesota was separately suing the federal government to obtain the same information.
  • Minnesota prosecutors had publicly asked: 'When was Jonathan Ross trained? What was he trained to do? What's in his personnel file?' — questions that had gone unanswered for three months.
  • The in camera review means only the judge would initially see the materials; they would not be immediately disclosed to the state or public.
mediaPlaceholder: Court order document listing the training and personnel records required for judicial review
imageAlt: Judge Orders Federal Government to Submit ICE Agent Ross's Training and Personnel Files for Private Review
